samples.xml_description:
<module id="multi_pipe_8bit">
    <description>Unsigned 8-bit multiplier implemented using pipelining processing. Utilizes a clock signal (clk), an active-low reset signal (rst_n), an input enable signal (mul_en_in), and provides an output enable signal (mul_en_out) and the product output (mul_out) of size 16 bits.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_en_in">Input enable signal that controls the multiplication operation.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[7:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 8 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_en_out">Output enable signal indicating if the multiplication operation is enabled.</output>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[15:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 16 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Implements an unsigned 8-bit multiplier using pipelining processing. The module utilizes a clock signal (clk), an active-low reset signal (rst_n), an input enable signal (mul_en_in), and provides an output enable signal (mul_en_out) and the product output (mul_out) of size 16 bits. The multiplication process involves generating partial products, calculating partial sums, and finally computing the final product. The output enable signal indicates whether the multiplication operation is enabled, while the product output reflects the result of the multiplication operation.</implementation>
</module>
